_comment: AgentGuard dependency safety registry — the source of truth for the
TestDependencyAudit_Gate check. A dependency fails the gate only when its
current version falls inside an advisory's affected range AND a fixed
release exists (an upgrade would resolve it).
kind = security  -> a known CVE / GHSA / Go vuln.
kind = performance -> a regression an upgrade fixes, evidenced by upstream
release notes or a benchmark (no automated DB exists
for these, which is exactly why this registry does).
affected[].introduced  first affected version ('0' = from the beginning).
affected[].fixed        first version that resolves it ('' / omitted = no
fix yet; reported as a note, does NOT fail the gate).
How to maintain: see advisories.md. Add an entry when govulncheck,
pip-audit, npm audit, or upstream notes surface an issue affecting a
version this project ships; remove it once every manifest has moved past
Entries below are real and historical; the project's pinned versions are
already past every 'fixed' boundary, so the gate is green. They exercise
the matcher end-to-end and document the schema.

advisories.summary: Denial of service: unmarshalling crafted YAML panics on a nil dereference.
